channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Byron Donalds' Campaign Faces Challenges Over Data Center Plans in Florida
item.description: An ex-GOP strategist warns that Republican gubernatorial candidate Byron Donalds faces a 'uniquely poisonous' issue that could derail his campaign. Despite receiving endorsements from President Trump ...

item.title: Trump's Tariffs Threaten British Columbia's Economy
item.description: The Trump administration's proposed tariffs are poised to significantly impact British Columbia's economy, particularly affecting manufacturers in the province. The Section 338 tariffs, set to take ef...

item.title: Jasper Philipsen claims Tour de France 17th stage victory after years of near misses
item.description: Belgian cyclist Jasper Philipsen secured his 11th Tour de France stage win on Wednesday, ending a frustrating streak of close calls. The 28-year-old Alpecin - Premier Tech rider dominated the reduced ...

item.title: African leaders call for AI investment to boost economic growth
item.description: African governments are being urged to prioritize investments in digital infrastructure, research, and skills development to harness the potential of artificial intelligence (AI) while ensuring ethica...

item.title: Tropical Depression Kiyapo Approaches Cagayan; PAGASA Issues Forecast Updates
item.description: The article details the development of Tropical Depression Kiyapo, which formed from a low-pressure area on July 22, 2026. PAGASA has issued forecasts indicating the system is expected to move toward ...

item.title: IBM Faces Mainframe Decline Amid AI-Driven Shifts
item.description: IBM reported a disappointing earnings quarter, with its mainframe business down 42% and overall revenue falling short of Wall Street expectations. CEO Arvind Krishna warned investors that the decline ...

item.title: Cyclospora Outbreaks Linked to Contaminated Lettuce and Food Safety Concerns
item.description: The article examines the recurring issue of cyclosporiasis outbreaks linked to leafy greens, particularly lettuce, highlighting how these foodborne illnesses are a persistent public health challenge. ...

item.title: Senator Robin Padilla Urges Prosecutors to Avoid One-Sided Approach in Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: During the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, Senator-judge Robin Padilla questioned House prosecutor Gerville Luistro about the credibility of witness Arturo Lascañas. Padilla criticiz...

item.title: Washington Nationals secure key win ahead of trade deadline
item.description: The Washington Nationals concluded their crucial road trip with a decisive victory over the Colorado Rockies, showcasing their dominance as the trade deadline approaches. The game, played on July 22, ...

item.title: Murder trial: Ferrel Govender faces charges over alleged threats and murder of businessman Shailen Singh
item.description: Ferrel Govender, former CEO of Pro Secure, is standing trial alongside his brother Darren for the December 2024 murder of businessman Shailen Singh. During court proceedings, a recording of Govender a...

item.title: Terrorists Attack Kwara Community, Kill Resident and Abduct Others as Security Forces Respond
item.description: Terrorists attacked Aboki Community in Kwara State, killing one resident and abducting two others. Security operatives and local vigilantes quickly responded, engaging the attackers and rescuing the k...

item.title: Canada introduces new graphic health warnings on tobacco products
item.description: Canada is implementing new graphic health warnings on cigarette packaging, set to take effect by August 1. The initiative includes 13 new warning labels featuring disturbing images such as bloody urin...

item.title: Nigeria partners with Germany to use AI for rural electrification
item.description: The Rural Electrification Agency (REA) has renewed its partnership with German tech firm VIDA to use AI-driven data analytics for Nigeria's $750 million rural electrification program. This collaborati...

item.title: Nigerian maritime lawyers push for urgent passage of port regulatory bill
item.description: Maritime lawyers in Nigeria have called on President Bola Ahmed Tinubu to expedite the approval of the Nigerian Port Economic Regulatory Agency Bill, emphasizing its critical role in modernizing the c...

item.title: Entrepreneurial couple Brian and Wendy balance business, marriage and parenthood
item.description: Entrepreneurs Brian Nguyen and Wendy Lin have built both a family and a business partnership, navigating the challenges of combining marriage with ambitious professional goals. The couple first met as...

item.title: President Samia restructures senior government roles in key ministries and agencies
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has reshuffled senior government officials, making new appointments and transfers across critical ministries. Key changes include Dr Latifa Mohammed Khamis becoming Deput...

item.title: NHRC probes excessive police action during student protests over unauthorized construction dispute
item.description: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) has initiated an inquiry into allegations of excessive use of force by law enforcement agencies against students participating in peaceful protests. The inc...

item.title: Trump's Affordability Joke Sparks Concern Over GOP's Midterm Strategy
item.description: President Donald Trump's joke at a rally in Marietta, Georgia, about the term 'affordability'—a word he claimed was coined by Democrats—has sparked significant criticism from political analysts. Durin...

item.title: Caitlin Clark's Absence from WNBA 3-Point Contest Sparks Concern Over League's All-Star Event
item.description: The WNBA faces significant challenges as Caitlin Clark, its most popular player, declines participation in the 3-Point Contest during the All-Star Weekend. This decision, along with similar rejections...

item.title: Supreme Court acquits man accused in 1996 blast after 23 years in jail
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has acquitted a man who was imprisoned for 23 years for his alleged involvement in a 1996 bomb blast case. The verdict, delivered after a prolonged legal battle, exonerated ...
